data.title: Uber Plows $500M into Self-Driving Startup Waabi to Power Thousands of New Vehicles
data.description:
Shares in ride-hailing group Uber Technologies (UBER) edged higher today after it plowed up to $500 million into a new partnership with Toronto-based autonomous vehicle startup Waabi.

Claim 50% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Power Uber Taxis

The Uber investment came as part of a new $1 billion funding round by Waabi, best known until now for its autonomous trucking technology.

The funding consists of an oversubscribed $750 million Series C round co-led by Khosla Ventures and G2 Venture Partners and roughly $250 million in milestone-based capital from Uber to support the deployment of 25,000 or more Waabi Driver-powered robotaxis exclusively on its platform. The companies did not provide a timeline for the deployment.  

It is understood that a further $250 million from Uber will be released later if Waabi meets specific robotaxi-expansion targets.

What really got tech experts purring was the ability of Waabi to tackle both the self-driving freight and robotaxis markets simultaneously. Rivals such as Alphabet's (GOOGL) Waymo have struggled to work on both at the same time, with Waymo closing its freight program back in 2023.

Next Level of Robotaxis

"Our incredible core technology really enables, for the first time, a single solution that can do multiple verticals, and they can do them at scale," Waabi founder, CEO Raquel Urtasun told TechCrunch. "It's not about two programs, two stacks."

She added that the Uber deal "brings the next level of scale to the robotaxi market."

In the last five years, Waabi has launched a series of commercial pilots with a human driver in the cab in Texas. It had planned to launch a fully driverless truck on public roads by the end of 2025, but that has been delayed to sometime in the next few quarters.

Other investors in the Series C round included NVentures, Nvidia's (NVDA) venture capital arm, and BlackRock (BLK).

Uber is certainly making a big bet on robotaxis.

Earlier this year Uber, along with EV maker Lucid (LCID) and autonomous driving company Nuro, said they were working together to launch a new robotaxi program using Lucid's Gravity SUV. The project was officially revealed at this year's Consumer Electronics Show (CES 2026) and will feature an in-cabin experience that has been designed entirely by Uber.
data.author: David Craik

data.title: SBUX Earnings: Starbucks Stock Rallies Despite Mixed Q1 2026 Results
data.description:
Starbucks (SBUX) stock was on the move Wednesday after the coffee chain posted its Fiscal Q1 2026 earnings report. The company reported adjusted earnings per share of 56 cents, which missed Wall Street's estimate of 59 cents. The company's adjusted EPS was also down 18.8% year-over-year from 69 cents.

Claim 50% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Starbucks reported revenue of $9.9 billion in its most recent earnings report, which was better than the $9.65 billion that analysts expected. It also represented a 5.5% year-over-year increase from the $9.34 billion reported in the same period of the year prior. This growth was fueled by a 4% increase in global comparable store sales, including a 4% growth in the U.S. comparable sales and a 5% growth in international comparable sales. This also marks the first time U.S. sales have increased in two years.

Starbucks stock was up 9.5% in pre-market trading on Wednesday, following a slight dip yesterday. Investors will note the stock has rallied 13.67% year-to-date but was still down 11.84% over the past 12 months.
data.author: William White

data.title: Why Is Tian Ruixiang Stock (TIRX) Up Today?
data.description:
Tian Ruixiang (TIRX) rocketed higher on Wednesday after the U.S. and Chinese insurance broker announced a Memorandum of Understanding (MOU) for a strategic investment from investor SwiftStart. This MOU will see SwiftStart purchase $80 million worth of TIRX stock priced at $1.50 per share, which is a 368.75% premium to the shares' closing price yesterday.

Claim 50% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

According to Tian Ruixiang, the execution of this MOU marks the first steps in a collaboration between it and SwiftStart. The two companies intend to explore further collaboration across multiple areas, including industry digital transformation, intelligent risk management systems, data asset operations, and international business expansion. The goal of this collaboration will be the enhancement of Tian Ruixiang's overall competitiveness in global capital markets and along the industry value chain.

Tian Ruixiang stated, "This cooperation framework provides additional strategic flexibility as we continue to strengthen our capital base and advance digital transformation initiatives, innovative business expansion, and potential mergers and acquisitions. We remain focused on improving user experience and operational efficiency to further reinforce our core competitive advantages."

Tian Ruixiang Stock Movement Today

Tian Ruixiang stock was up 117.13% in pre-market trading on Wednesday, following a 27.42% fall yesterday. The shares have decreased 10.83% year-to-date and 96.33% over the past 12 months.

With today's news came heavy trading of TIRX stock, as more than 94 million shares changed hands, compared to a three-month daily average of about 298,000 units.
data.author: William White

data.title: LMT Stock: Watch These KPIs Ahead of Lockheed Martin's Q4 Results
data.description:
Lockheed Martin (LMT), a leading aerospace and defense contractor, will release its fourth-quarter fiscal 2025 results before the market opens on Thursday, January 29. Investors should track Lockheed Martin's key performance indicators (KPIs) as they reveal past results and shape future expectations.

Claim 50% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

A KPI is a quantifiable metric like revenue growth, margins, or daily active users that provides insight into trends, efficiency, and potential challenges such as market changes or resource inefficiencies.

LMT's Prime KPI: Program Backlog

Lockheed Martin's program backlog reflects future revenue potential from secured contracts and its ability to win new orders exceeding current sales. Breaking it down by business segment highlights areas of strongest demand.

In Q3FY25, LMT's total backlog reached $179.08 billion, driven by strong international demand for the F-35 and anticipated awards in key programs like JASSM, LARASM, and PAC-3.
data.author: Sheryl Sheth

data.title: Meta (META) Explores Subscription Revenue Across Instagram, Facebook, and WhatsApp
data.description:
Meta Platforms (META) is preparing to test paid plans across Instagram, Facebook, and WhatsApp in the coming months. According to comments shared with TechCrunch, the company says these plans will offer extra features while keeping the free versions active for all users. Meta said the "core experiences" will remain the same for users who stay on the ad-based tier. This news comes as the company prepares to release its fourth-quarter results today after the market closes.

Claim 50% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

At first glance, this move may sound similar to streaming services like Netflix (NFLX) or Spotify (SPOT). However, the business logic is very different. Meta already earns most of its money from ads, not from user fees. In the third quarter, the company reported total revenue of $51.2 billion, with about $50 billion or nearly 98% coming from advertising. As a result, any paid plan would sit on top of a very strong ad business rather than replace it.

Meanwhile, META shares moved slightly on Tuesday, closing at $672.97.
data.author: Ran Melamed

data.title: Intel Stock Skyrockets as Nvidia and Apple Partner with Intel to Diversify 2028 Chip Production Away from TSMC
data.description:
Industry giants Nvidia (NVDA) and Apple (AAPL) are preparing to move portions of their 2028 semiconductor production to Intel, according to an exclusive report from DIGITIMES Asia. This development signals a significant pivot in the global supply chain, as American tech leaders respond to mounting political pressure and manufacturing mandates from the Trump administration.

Claim 50% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

While TSMC (TSM) remains the primary partner for high-end cores, both companies are reportedly targeting low-volume, low-tier, non-core projects for Intel to diversify their risks and satisfy domestic manufacturing requirements.

Nvidia Integrates Intel into Feynman Supply Chain

The upcoming Feynman architecture, the successor to Nvidia's Rubin series, will reportedly be the first major beneficiary of this dual-foundry approach. While the critical GPU die remains at TSMC, Nvidia plans to shift portions of the I/O die to Intel's 14A process by 2028. This shift follows a $5 billion investment Nvidia made in Intel in late 2025, signaling a long-term commitment to building a Made-in-America secondary supply route.

Advanced packaging is a key component of this partnership. Intel is expected to manage up to 25% of the final packaging for these chips using its EMIB (Embedded Multi-die Interconnect Bridge) technology. TSMC will continue to manage the remaining 75%, ensuring that Nvidia's most complex work stays with its most proven partner.

Apple Rekindles Intel Manufacturing Relationship

Apple is also exploring a return to Intel's fabs, though the focus is strictly on entry-level M-series processors for MacBooks. This marks the first major collaboration between the two since Apple completed its transition to in-house Apple Silicon in 2022. The current move is largely a response to Trump administration policies and the need to diversify away from a total reliance on overseas suppliers.

TSMC Gains Relief from Order Diversions

Experts believe this move actually helps TSMC instead of hurting them. TSMC can lower the political heat it feels over being "too big" if it lets Intel handle these smaller, less important orders. This plan also makes it less likely for the government to investigate them for being a monopoly. Most importantly, it frees up their limited factory space to focus on the most expensive and profitable chips for their best customers.

Investors can compare AI stocks side-by-side on the TipRanks Stocks Comparison Tool. Click on the image below to find out more.
data.author: Annika Masrani

data.title: U.S. Stock Futures Trade Mixed Ahead of Big Tech Earnings, Fed's Rate Decision
data.description:
U.S. stock futures traded mixed on Wednesday morning ahead of the Federal Reserve's interest rate decision and earnings from major technology companies. Futures on the Nasdaq 100 (NDX) and the S&P 500 (SPX) were up 0.80% and 0.19%, respectively, while futures tied to the Dow Jones Industrial Average (DJIA) declined 0.03% at 8:36 a.m. EST on January 28.

Claim 50% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

During Tuesday's regular trading, the three indexes closed mixed, with the Dow dropping 0.83%. Shares of UnitedHealth (UNH) plunged 19.6%, dragging down the index. UNH fell despite reporting robust Q4 FY25 revenues, as news of a Medicare Advantage payment increase of just 0.09% for 2027 pressured the stock. Meanwhile, the S&P 500 gained 0.41% to finish at a record high, and the Nasdaq Composite climbed 0.91%.

On the economic front, all eyes are on the Federal Reserve's first interest-rate decision today. The central bank is widely expected to hold interest rates steady, while investors look for clues about future policy directions.

Turning to trending stocks, U.S.-listed shares of ASML (ASML) soared more than 5% after the chip giant reported record orders and issued robust 2026 guidance due AI-led demand. Seagate Technology (STX) stock jumped 10% in pre-market trading after the company reported impressive Q2 FY26 earnings and issued a solid guidance, driven by strong demand for its storage devices amid the AI boom. Nvidia (NVDA) and Advanced Micro Devices (AMD) shares trended higher after Reuters reported that China has given approval to ByteDance, Alibaba (BABA), and Tencent (TCHEY) to purchase Nvidia's H200 AI chips.

data.title: Stock Market News Today, 1/28/26 – U.S. Stock Futures Rise Ahead of Interest Rate Decision, Big Tech Earnings
data.description:
U.S. stock futures rose early Wednesday as traders anticipated the Federal Reserve's interest rate decision and earnings from three major technology companies. Futures on the Nasdaq 100 (NDX), the S&P 500 (SPX), and the Dow Jones Industrial Average (DJIA) were up 0.86%, 0.33%, and 0.02%, respectively, at 3:27 a.m. EST on January 28.

Claim 50% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

During Tuesday's regular trading, the three indexes closed mixed, with the Dow dropping 0.83%. Shares of UnitedHealth (UNH) plunged 19.6%, dragging down the index. UNH fell despite reporting robust Q4FY25 revenues, as news of a Medicare Advantage payment increase of just 0.09% for 2027 pressured the stock. Meanwhile, the S&P 500 gained 0.41% to finish at a record high, and the Nasdaq Composite climbed 0.91%.

On the economic front, all eyes are on the Federal Reserve's first interest-rate decision today. The central bank is widely expected to hold interest rates steady, while investors look for clues about future policy directions.

Turning to earnings season, major companies reporting today include Microsoft (MSFT), Meta Platforms (META), Tesla (TSLA), IBM (IBM), AT&T (T), and Starbucks (SBUX).

Notably, the U.S. 10-year Treasury yield was up, floating near 4.23%. WTI crude oil futures were trending higher, hovering near $62.48 per barrel as of the last check. Additionally, the Gold Spot U.S. dollar price increased to nearly $5,290 per ounce on Wednesday.

Elsewhere, European indexes opened mixed today amid expectations of a slew of corporate earnings releases.

Asia-Pacific Markets Trade Higher

Asia-Pacific markets traded mostly higher on January 28, tracking the S&P 500's gains.

Hong Kong's Hang Seng Index added 2.43%. In China, the Shanghai Composite added 0.27%, while the Shenzhen Component rose 0.21%. Meanwhile, Japan's Nikkei rose 0.05%, while the Topix fell 0.79%.
